question: Network Address Translation (NAT)
canonical_answer:
- Translates a private address into a public address
- Hides devices in a private network
- Allows sharing of a single public IP address or a pool of public IP addresses
Dynamic
Static NAT

appears_in.title: Comptia A+ Core 1 Study Guide
appears_in.answer: Dynamically assigns IP address information (for example, IP address, subnet mask, DNS server's IP address, and default gateway's IP address) to network devices.

appears_in.title: Comptia A+ Free Study Guide
appears_in.answer: A TCP/IP protocol developed as a solution to the dwindling number of IP addresses on the Internet and that also serves to hide IP addresses on a private network from the Internet.

appears_in.title: Comptia A+ Material
appears_in.answer: A TCP/IP protocol developed as a solution to the dwindling number of IP addresses on the Internet and that also serves to hide IP addresses on a private network from the Internet.

appears_in.title: Comptia Fundamentals Study Guide
appears_in.answer: A service that translates private, nonroutable IP addresses into public addresses that can be used on the Internet.

appears_in.title: Comptia Security+ Study Guide

- Allows sharing of a single public IP address or a pool of public IP addresses
